# useFilter

Synchronizes typed filter state with the current URL through debounced Inertia visits.

## Contents

- [Usage](#usage)
- [URL and visit behavior](#url-and-visit-behavior)
- [State and resetting](#state-and-resetting)
- [API Reference](#api-reference)

## Usage

Define every supported filter key and its default value. The composable reads matching values from the current query string and returns a reactive object that can be bound directly to controls.

*CustomersFilter.vue*

```vue
<template>
  <Input v-model="filter.search" placeholder="Search customers" />
  <CheckboxControl v-model="filter.premium">Premium only</CheckboxControl>
  <Button v-if="filter.applied" variant="ghost" @click="filter.reset()">
    Reset
  </Button>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { useFilter } from '@stacktrace/ui'

const filter = useFilter({
  search: '',
  premium: false,
}, {
  onSuccess: () => console.info('Results updated'),
})
</script>
```

## URL and visit behavior

- Changes are debounced for 50 milliseconds and cancel an older pending visit.
- Visits preserve component state and scroll position.
- Values equal to their defaults are removed from the query string.
- Empty strings, empty arrays, `false`, `null`, and `undefined` are treated as equivalent empty values.
- Changing filters removes existing `page` and `cursor` parameters.

## State and resetting

Read `filter.applied` to show a reset action. Use `filter.appliedOnly(keys)` when one control represents only part of the filter, and call `filter.reset()` to restore all defaults. The `data()` method returns only declared filter keys, excluding helper properties.

## API Reference

### `useFilter`

Creates URL-backed reactive filter state from an object or a factory that returns fresh defaults.

```typescript
function useFilter<TFilter extends object>(
  state: TFilter | (() => TFilter),
  options?: Partial<FilterOptions>,
): Filter<TFilter>
```

#### Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `state` (required) | `TFilter \| (() => TFilter)` | Declares the supported filter keys and their default values. |
| `options` | `Partial<FilterOptions>` | Provides optional lifecycle behavior for successful Inertia visits. |

#### Returns

`Filter<TFilter>` — Reactive filter values combined with URL-filter helper properties and methods.

### `FilterData`

The base constraint for an object containing declared filter values.

```typescript
type FilterData = object
```

### `FilterProps`

Helper state and operations added to the declared filter values.

```typescript
interface FilterProps<TFilter extends FilterData> {
  data(): TFilter
  reset(): void
  applied: boolean
  appliedOnly(keys: Array<keyof TFilter>): boolean
}
```

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data` | `() => TFilter` | Returns only the filter keys declared by the initial state. |
| `reset` | `() => void` | Restores every filter key to its declared default. |
| `applied` | `boolean` | Indicates whether any filter value differs from its default. |
| `appliedOnly` | `(keys: Array<keyof TFilter>) => boolean` | Checks whether any selected filter key differs from its default. |

### `Filter`

The reactive result returned by useFilter: application values intersected with filter helpers.

```typescript
type Filter<TFilter extends FilterData> = TFilter & FilterProps<TFilter>
```

### `FilterOptions`

Optional callbacks accepted while creating a filter.

```typescript
interface FilterOptions {
  onSuccess: VoidFunction
}
```

| Property | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `onSuccess` | `VoidFunction` | Runs after a filter-triggered Inertia visit succeeds. |

Hosted documentation: [useFilter](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/composables/filter)
